1. Any existing definitions of "distinct subset products" or "distinct subset sums" (similar concepts)
2. Any existing prime counting function definitions (primeCounting, primeCountingFunction, Nat.primeCounting, etc.)
3. Any existing definitions related to maximal set sizes with product/sum constraints
4. Check FormalConjecturesForMathlib/ directory for relevant utilities
5. Check if Mathlib has a prime counting function (look for `Nat.primeCounting` or similar)
6. Look for any other ErdŇĎs problems that deal with subset products or similar combinatorial number theory

Search in: FormalConjectures/, FormalConjecturesForMathlib/, and check imports. Be very thorough.

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 795
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/795](https://www.erdosproblems.com/795)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $g(n)$ be the maximal size of $A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, n\}$ such that the subset products
    25‚Üí$\prod_{a \in S} a$ are distinct for all $S \subseteq A$. ErdŇĎs proved that
    26‚Üí$g(n) \leq \pi(n) + O(\sqrt{n} / \log n)$. This upper bound is essentially best possible,
    27‚Üísince one could take $A$ to be all primes and squares of primes.
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[Ra25] Raghavan, S., _On the ErdŇĎs distinct subset products problem_, 2025.
    30‚Üí-/
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üíopen Finset BigOperators
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üínamespace Erdos795
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí/-- A finset of natural numbers has distinct subset products if for all subsets
    37‚Üí$S, T \subseteq A$, $\prod_{a \in S} a = \prod_{a \in T} a$ implies $S = T$. -/
    38‚Üídef HasDistinctSubsetProducts (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    39‚Üí  ‚ąÄ S T, S ‚äÜ A ‚Üí T ‚äÜ A ‚Üí (‚ąŹ i ‚ąą S, i) = (‚ąŹ i ‚ąą T, i) ‚Üí S = T
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/-- The prime counting function $\pi(n)$: the number of primes $\leq n$. -/
    42‚Üídef primeCounting (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    43‚Üí  ((Finset.range (n + 1)).filter Nat.Prime).card
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- $g(n)$: the maximal cardinality of $A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, n\}$ with distinct subset
    46‚Üíproducts. -/
    47‚Üínoncomputable def maxDistinctProductSetSize (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    48‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ A : Finset ‚Ąē, (‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, 1 ‚Č§ a ‚ąß a ‚Č§ n) ‚ąß
    49‚Üí    HasDistinctSubsetProducts A ‚ąß A.card = k}
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/--
    52‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 795: $g(n) \leq \pi(n) + \pi(\lfloor\sqrt{n}\rfloor) + o(\sqrt{n} / \log n)$.
    53‚Üí
    54‚ÜíFormulated as: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
    55‚Üí$g(n) \leq \pi(n) + \pi(\lfloor\sqrt{n}\rfloor) + \varepsilon \cdot \sqrt{n} / \log n$.
    56‚Üí
    57‚ÜíThis was solved by Raghavan [Ra25], who proved
    58‚Üí$g(n) \leq \pi(n) + \pi(\lfloor\sqrt{n}\rfloor) + O(n^{5/12 + o(1)})$.
    59‚Üí-/
    60‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    61‚Üítheorem erdos_795 :
    62‚Üí    answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    63‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    64‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    65‚Üí      (maxDistinctProductSetSize n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§
    66‚Üí        (primeCounting n : ‚ĄĚ) + (primeCounting (Nat.sqrt n) : ‚ĄĚ) +
    67‚Üí        őĶ * (Nat.sqrt n : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    68‚Üí  sorry
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üíend Erdos795
    71‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 121
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/121](https://www.erdosproblems.com/121)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $F_k(N)$ be the size of the largest subset of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ with no $k$ distinct
    25‚Üíelements whose product is a perfect square. ErdŇĎs‚ÄďS√≥s‚ÄďS√°rk√∂zy conjectured
    26‚Üí$F_{2k+1}(N) = (1 - o(1))N$ for $k \geq 2$; disproved by Tao, who showed $F_k(N) \leq
    27‚Üí(1 - c_k)N$ for all $k \geq 4$.
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[Er94b] [Er97] [Er97e] [Er98] ErdŇĎs, P., various problems papers.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[ESS95] ErdŇĎs, P., S√°rk√∂zy, A. and S√≥s, V. T., on products of distinct elements of subsets.
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[Er38] ErdŇĎs, P., on sequences of integers no one of which divides the product of two others
    34‚Üíand on some related problems (1938).
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí[Ta24] Tao, T., on the density of sets avoiding square-product patterns (2024).
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üíopen scoped BigOperators
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üíopen Filter
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üínamespace Erdos121
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- A finset $A$ has the property that no $k$ distinct elements have a product that is
    46‚Üía perfect square. -/
    47‚Üídef NoKSquareProduct (k : ‚Ąē) (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    48‚Üí  ‚ąÄ B : Finset ‚Ąē, B ‚äÜ A ‚Üí B.card = k ‚Üí ¬¨IsSquare (‚ąŹ b ‚ąą B, b)
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- $F(k, N)$ is the size of the largest subset $A$ of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ such that
    51‚Üíno $k$ distinct elements of $A$ have a product that is a perfect square. -/
    52‚Üínoncomputable def F (k N : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    53‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ A : Finset ‚Ąē, A ‚äÜ Finset.Icc 1 N ‚ąß A.card = m ‚ąß NoKSquareProduct k A}
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/--
    56‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem #121 [Er94b, Er97, Er97e, Er98] ‚ÄĒ DISPROVED
    57‚Üí
    58‚ÜíLet $F_k(N)$ be the size of the largest $A \subseteq \{1,\ldots,N\}$ such that the product
    59‚Üíof no $k$ distinct elements of $A$ is a perfect square.
    60‚Üí
    61‚ÜíConjectured by ErdŇĎs, S√≥s, and S√°rk√∂zy [ESS95]: Is $F_{2k+1}(N) = (1 - o(1)) N$ for all
    62‚Üí$k \geq 2$? Answered in the negative by Tao [Ta24].
    63‚Üí-/
    64‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    65‚Üítheorem erdos_121 : answer(False) ‚ÜĒ
    66‚Üí    ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, 2 ‚Č§ k ‚Üí
    67‚Üí      Tendsto (fun N => (F (2 * k + 1) N : ‚ĄĚ) / (N : ‚ĄĚ)) atTop (nhds 1) := by
    68‚Üí  sorry
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/--
    71‚ÜíTao [Ta24] proved that for any $k \geq 4$ there exists a constant $c_k > 0$ such that
    72‚Üí$F_k(N) \leq (1 - c_k + o(1)) N$, disproving the conjecture for all odd $k \geq 5$.
    73‚Üí-/
    74‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    75‚Üítheorem erdos_121.variants.tao_upper_bound :
    76‚Üí    ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, 4 ‚Č§ k ‚Üí
    77‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < c ‚ąß
    78‚Üí      ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < őĶ ‚Üí
    79‚Üí        ‚ąÄŠ∂† N : ‚Ąē in atTop, (F k N : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ (1 - c + őĶ) * N := by
    80‚Üí  sorry

    82‚Üíend Erdos121

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 490
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíIf $A, B \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ are such that all products $ab$ ($a \in A$, $b \in B$)
    23‚Üíare distinct, is it true that $|A| \cdot |B| \ll N^2 / \log N$?
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/490](https://www.erdosproblems.com/490)
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Sz76] Szemer√©di, E., *On the number of edges in products of sets of integers*. 1976.
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üínamespace Erdos490
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí/--
    33‚ÜíThe products $ab$ with $a \in A$ and $b \in B$ are all distinct: the multiplication
    34‚Üímap $A \times B \to \mathbb{N}$ is injective.
    35‚Üí-/
    36‚Üídef HasDistinctProducts (A B : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=

    38‚Üí    a‚āĀ * b‚āĀ = a‚āā * b‚āā ‚Üí a‚āĀ = a‚āā ‚ąß b‚āĀ = b‚āā
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/--
    41‚ÜíIf $A, B \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ are such that all products $ab$ ($a \in A$, $b \in B$)
    42‚Üíare distinct, then $|A| \cdot |B| \ll N^2 / \log N$.
    43‚Üí
    44‚ÜíFormally: there exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all $N$ and all
    45‚Üí$A, B \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ with distinct products,
    46‚Üí$$
    47‚Üí  |A| \cdot |B| \le C \cdot \frac{N^2}{\log N}.
    48‚Üí$$
    49‚Üí
    50‚ÜíProved by Szemer√©di [Sz76].
    51‚Üí-/
    52‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    53‚Üítheorem erdos_490 :
    54‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß
    55‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (N : ‚Ąē) (A B : Finset ‚Ąē),
    56‚Üí        (‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, 1 ‚Č§ a ‚ąß a ‚Č§ N) ‚Üí
    57‚Üí        (‚ąÄ b ‚ąą B, 1 ‚Č§ b ‚ąß b ‚Č§ N) ‚Üí
    58‚Üí        HasDistinctProducts A B ‚Üí
    59‚Üí        (A.card : ‚ĄĚ) * (B.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ C * ((N : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 / Real.log (N : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
    60‚Üí  sorry
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üíend Erdos490
    63‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 896
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/896](https://www.erdosproblems.com/896)
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí[Er72] ErdŇĎs, P., _Quelques probl√®mes de th√©orie des nombres_, p. 81, 1972.
    25‚Üí
    26‚ÜíEstimate the maximum of $F(A,B)$ as $A, B$ range over all subsets of $\{1,\ldots,N\}$,
    27‚Üíwhere $F(A,B)$ counts the number of $m$ such that $m = ab$ has exactly one solution with
    28‚Üí$a \in A$ and $b \in B$.
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíVan Doorn proved:
    31‚Üí$$
    32‚Üí(1 + o(1)) \frac{N^2}{\log N} \leq \max_{A,B} F(A,B) \ll \frac{N^2}{(\log N)^\delta (\log \log N)^{3/2}}
    33‚Üí$$
    34‚Üíwhere $\delta = 1 - \frac{1 + \log \log 2}{\log 2} \approx 0.086$.
    35‚Üí
    36‚ÜíThe open problem is to determine the exact asymptotic order.
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üíopen Finset Real
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üínamespace Erdos896
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/--
    44‚Üí$F(A,B)$ counts the number of distinct products $m = a \cdot b$ (with $a \in A$, $b \in B$)
    45‚Üíthat have exactly one representation as such a product.
    46‚Üí-/
    47‚Üídef uniqueProductCount (A B : Finset ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    48‚Üí  ((A √óňĘ B).image (fun p => p.1 * p.2)).filter (fun m =>
    49‚Üí    ((A √óňĘ B).filter (fun p => p.1 * p.2 = m)).card = 1) |>.card
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/-- The maximum of $F(A,B)$ over all $A, B \subseteq \{1,\ldots,N\}$ is $O(N^2 / \log N)$,
    52‚Üíi.e., the lower bound gives the correct order of magnitude. [Er72] -/
    53‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    54‚Üítheorem erdos_896 :
    55‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < C ‚ąß
    56‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, N‚āÄ ‚Č§ N ‚Üí
    57‚Üí      ‚ąÄ A B : Finset ‚Ąē, A ‚äÜ Finset.Icc 1 N ‚Üí B ‚äÜ Finset.Icc 1 N ‚Üí
    58‚Üí        (uniqueProductCount A B : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ C * (N : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 / Real.log (N : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    59‚Üí  sorry
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí/-- Known lower bound (van Doorn): for any $\varepsilon > 0$ and all sufficiently large $N$,
    62‚Üíthere exist $A, B \subseteq \{1,\ldots,N\}$ with
    63‚Üí$F(A,B) \geq (1-\varepsilon) \cdot N^2 / \log N$. [Er72] -/
    64‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    65‚Üítheorem erdos_896.variants.lower_bound :
    66‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < őĶ ‚Üí
    67‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, N‚āÄ ‚Č§ N ‚Üí
    68‚Üí      ‚ąÉ A B : Finset ‚Ąē, A ‚äÜ Finset.Icc 1 N ‚ąß B ‚äÜ Finset.Icc 1 N ‚ąß
    69‚Üí        (uniqueProductCount A B : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• (1 - őĶ) * (N : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 / Real.log (N : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    70‚Üí  sorry
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üíend Erdos896

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 350
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*References:*
    23‚Üí- [erdosproblems.com/350](https://www.erdosproblems.com/350)
    24‚Üí- [BeEr74] Benkoski, S. J. and ErdŇĎs, P., On weird and pseudoperfect numbers. Math. Comp. (1974),
    25‚Üí  617-623.
    26‚Üí- [HSS77] Hanson, F. and Steele, J. M. and Stenger, F., Distinct sums over subsets. Proc. Amer.
    27‚Üí  Math. Soc. (1977), 179-180.
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üínamespace Erdos350
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí/-- The predicate that all (finite) subsets of `A` have distinct sums. -/
    33‚Üídef DistinctSubsetSums {M : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] (A : Set M) : Prop :=
    34‚Üí  Set.Pairwise {X : Finset M | ‚ÜĎX ‚äÜ A} fun X Y => X.sum id ‚Č† Y.sum id
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí/-- The predicate that all (finite) subsets of `A` have distinct sums, decidable version -/
    37‚Üídef DecidableDistinctSubsetSums {M : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [DecidableEq M] (A : Finset M) : Prop :=
    38‚Üí  ‚ąÄ X ‚äÜ A, ‚ąÄ Y ‚äÜ A, X ‚Č† Y ‚Üí X.sum id ‚Č† Y.sum id
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí@[category test, AMS 5 11]
    41‚Üítheorem decidableDistinctSubsetSums_1_2 : DecidableDistinctSubsetSums {1, 2} := by
    42‚Üí  rw [DecidableDistinctSubsetSums] ; decide
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí@[category test, AMS 5 11]
    45‚Üítheorem distinctSubsetSums_1_2 : DistinctSubsetSums ({1, 2} : Set ‚Ąē) := by
    46‚Üí  simp only [DistinctSubsetSums, Set.Pairwise, Set.mem_setOf_eq, ne_eq, id_eq]
    47‚Üí  intro x hx y hy hxy
    48‚Üí  -- FIXME: Why is `norm_cast` useless here?
    49‚Üí  simp_rw [‚Üź Finset.coe_singleton, ‚Üź Finset.coe_insert, Finset.coe_subset, ‚ÜźFinset.mem_powerset] at *
    50‚Üí  fin_cases hx <;> fin_cases hy <;> simp_all
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/-- Small sanity check: the two predicates are saying the same thing. -/
    53‚Üí@[category API, AMS 5 11]
    54‚Üítheorem DistinctSubsetSums_iff_DecidableDistinctSubsetSums
    55‚Üí    {M : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [DecidableEq M] (A : Finset M) :
    56‚Üí    DistinctSubsetSums (A : Set M) ‚ÜĒ DecidableDistinctSubsetSums A := by
    57‚Üí  rw [DistinctSubsetSums, DecidableDistinctSubsetSums, Set.Pairwise] ; simp_all
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí/--
    60‚ÜíIf `A ‚äā ‚Ąē` is a finite set of integers all of whose subset sums are distinct then `‚ąĎ n ‚ąą A, 1/n < 2`.
    61‚ÜíProved by Ryavec.
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíThis was proved by Ryavec, who did not appear to ever publish the proof. Ryavec's proof is
    64‚Üíreproduced in [BeEr74]. More generally, Ryavec's proof delivers that
    65‚Üí$\sum_{n\in A}\frac{1}{n}\leq 2-2^{1-\lvert A\rvert},$ with equality if and only if
    66‚Üí$A=\{1,2,\ldots,2^k\}$.
    67‚Üí
    68‚ÜíThis was formalized in Lean by Alexeev using Aristotle.
    69‚Üí-/
    70‚Üí@[category research formally solved using lean4 at
    71‚Üí"https://github.com/plby/lean-proofs/blob/main/src/v4.24.0/ErdosProblems/Erdos350.lean", AMS 5 11]
    72‚Üítheorem erdos_350 (A : Finset ‚Ąē) (hA : DecidableDistinctSubsetSums A) :
    73‚Üí    ‚ąĎ n ‚ąą A, (1 / n : ‚ĄĚ) < 2 := by
    74‚Üí  sorry
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/--
    77‚ÜíIf `A ‚äā ‚Ąē` is a finite set of integers all of whose subset sums are distinct then `‚ąĎ n ‚ąą A, 1/n^s < 1/(1 - 2^(-s))`, for any `s > 0`.
    78‚ÜíProved by Hanson, Steele, and Stenger [HSS77].
    79‚Üí
    80‚ÜíWe exlude here the case `s = 0`, because in the informal formulation then the right hand side is to be interpreted as `‚ąě`, while the left hand side counts the elements in `A`.
    81‚Üí-/
    82‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    83‚Üítheorem erdos_350.variants.strengthening (A : Finset ‚Ąē) (hA : DecidableDistinctSubsetSums A)
    84‚Üí    (s : ‚ĄĚ) (hs : 0 < s) : ‚ąĎ n ‚ąą A, (1 / n : ‚ĄĚ)^s < 1 / (1 - 2^(-s)) := by
    85‚Üí  sorry
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üíend Erdos350

    11‚Üídistributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12‚ÜíWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 425
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíLet $F(n)$ be the maximum size of a multiplicative Sidon subset of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$
    23‚Üí(where all pairwise products are distinct). The problem asks whether the error term
    24‚Üí$F(n) - \pi(n)$ has a precise asymptotic of the form $c \cdot n^{3/4} (\log n)^{-3/2}$
    25‚Üífor some constant $c > 0$, and poses a generalization to $r$-fold products.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/425](https://www.erdosproblems.com/425)
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[Er68] ErdŇĎs, P., _On some properties of prime factors of integers_. Nagoya Math. J. (1968).
    30‚Üí-/
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üíopen Filter
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üínamespace Erdos425
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí/--
    37‚ÜíA finite set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is a *multiplicative $B_2$ set* (or multiplicative Sidon set)
    38‚Üíif whenever $ab = cd$ for $a, b, c, d \in A$ with $a < b$ and $c < d$, then $a = c$ and $b = d$.
    39‚ÜíEquivalently, the pairwise products $\{ab : a, b \in A, a < b\}$ are all distinct.
    40‚Üí-/
    41‚Üídef IsMultB2 (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    42‚Üí  ‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ d ‚ąą A,
    43‚Üí    a < b ‚Üí c < d ‚Üí a * b = c * d ‚Üí a = c ‚ąß b = d
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/--
    46‚ÜíA finite set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an *$r$-multiplicative Sidon set* if any two $r$-element
    47‚Üísubsets of $A$ with equal products must be the same subset.
    48‚Üí-/
    49‚Üídef IsMultBr (r : ‚Ąē) (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    50‚Üí  ‚ąÄ S T : Finset ‚Ąē, S ‚äÜ A ‚Üí T ‚äÜ A ‚Üí S.card = r ‚Üí T.card = r ‚Üí
    51‚Üí    S.prod id = T.prod id ‚Üí S = T
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/--
    54‚Üí$F(n)$ is the maximum cardinality of a multiplicative $B_2$ subset of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$.
    55‚Üí-/
    56‚Üínoncomputable def multB2MaxSize (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    57‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ A : Finset ‚Ąē, IsMultB2 A ‚ąß (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą A, 1 ‚Č§ x ‚ąß x ‚Č§ n) ‚ąß A.card = k}
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí/--
    60‚ÜíLet $F(n)$ be the maximum size of a multiplicative $B_2$ subset of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$
    61‚Üí(a set where all pairwise products $ab$ with $a < b$ are distinct).
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíErdŇĎs [Er68] proved that there exist constants $0 < c_1 \leq c_2$ such that
    64‚Üí$$
    65‚Üí  \pi(n) + c_1 n^{3/4} (\log n)^{-3/2} \leq F(n) \leq \pi(n) + c_2 n^{3/4} (\log n)^{-3/2}.
    66‚Üí$$
    67‚Üí
    68‚ÜíThe conjecture asks whether there exists a constant $c$ such that
    69‚Üí$$
    70‚Üí  F(n) = \pi(n) + (c + o(1))\, n^{3/4} (\log n)^{-3/2},
    71‚Üí$$
    72‚Üíi.e., whether the ratio $(F(n) - \pi(n)) / (n^{3/4} / (\log n)^{3/2})$ converges.
    73‚Üí-/
    74‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    75‚Üítheorem erdos_425 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    76‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, c > 0 ‚ąß
    77‚Üí      Tendsto
    78‚Üí        (fun n : ‚Ąē =>
    79‚Üí          ((multB2MaxSize n : ‚ĄĚ) - (Nat.primeCounting n : ‚ĄĚ)) /
    80‚Üí          ((n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((3 : ‚ĄĚ) / 4) / (Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ)) ^ ((3 : ‚ĄĚ) / 2)))
    81‚Üí        atTop
    82‚Üí        (nhds c) := by
    83‚Üí  sorry
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/--
    86‚ÜíIf $A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, n\}$ is such that all products $a_1 \cdots a_r$ are distinct for
    87‚Üí$a_1 < \cdots < a_r$ (i.e., $A$ is an $r$-multiplicative Sidon set), then
    88‚Üí$$
    89‚Üí  |A| \leq \pi(n) + O(n^{(r+1)/(2r)}).
    90‚Üí$$
    91‚Üí-/
    92‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    93‚Üítheorem erdos_425.variants.part2 :
    94‚Üí    ‚ąÄ r : ‚Ąē, 2 ‚Č§ r ‚Üí
    95‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß
    96‚Üí      ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ A : Finset ‚Ąē,
    97‚Üí        (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą A, 1 ‚Č§ x ‚ąß x ‚Č§ n) ‚Üí
    98‚Üí        IsMultBr r A ‚Üí
    99‚Üí        (A.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ (Nat.primeCounting n : ‚ĄĚ) +
   100‚Üí          C * (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ (((r : ‚ĄĚ) + 1) / (2 * (r : ‚ĄĚ))) := by
   101‚Üí  sorry
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üíend Erdos425
   104‚Üí

    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 841
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/841](https://www.erdosproblems.com/841)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $t_n$ be minimal such that $\{n+1, \ldots, n+t_n\}$ contains a subset whose
    25‚Üíproduct with $n$ is a square number (and let $t_n = 0$ if $n$ is itself square).
    26‚ÜíEstimate $t_n$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs, Graham, and Selfridge.
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[ErSe92] ErdŇĎs, P. and Selfridge, J. L., 1992.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[BPZ24] Bui, H. M., Pratt, K., and Zaharescu, A., 2024.
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üíopen Finset
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üínamespace Erdos841
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- The set $\{n+1, \ldots, n+t\}$ contains a nonempty subset whose product with $n$
    40‚Üíis a perfect square. -/
    41‚Üídef HasSquareProductSubset (n t : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    42‚Üí  ‚ąÉ S : Finset ‚Ąē, S.Nonempty ‚ąß S ‚äÜ Finset.Icc (n + 1) (n + t) ‚ąß
    43‚Üí    IsSquare (n * S.prod id)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- $t_n$: the minimal $t$ such that $\{n+1, \ldots, n+t\}$ contains a nonempty subset whose
    46‚Üíproduct with $n$ is a perfect square. Defined as $0$ when $n$ is a perfect square. -/
    47‚Üínoncomputable def erdos841T (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    48‚Üí  if IsSquare n then 0
    49‚Üí  else sInf {t : ‚Ąē | HasSquareProductSubset n t}
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/-- The largest prime factor of $n$, or $0$ if $n \leq 1$. -/
    52‚Üínoncomputable def largestPrimeFactor (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    53‚Üí  (Nat.primeFactors n).sup id
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/--
    56‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 841** ‚ÄĒ Trivial lower bound:
    57‚Üí
    58‚ÜíFor all non-square $n \geq 2$, $t_n \geq P(n)$ where $P(n)$ is the largest prime factor of $n$.
    59‚Üí-/
    60‚Üí@[category undergraduate, AMS 11]
    61‚Üítheorem erdos_841 (n : ‚Ąē) (hn : n ‚Č• 2) (hns : ¬¨IsSquare n) :
    62‚Üí    erdos841T n ‚Č• largestPrimeFactor n := by
    63‚Üí  sorry
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí/--
    66‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 841** ‚ÄĒ Selfridge's result [ErSe92]:
    67‚Üí
    68‚ÜíIf the largest prime factor $P(n) > \sqrt{2n} + 1$, then $t_n = P(n)$.
    69‚Üí-/
    70‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    71‚Üítheorem erdos_841.variants.selfridge (n : ‚Ąē) (hn : n ‚Č• 2) (hns : ¬¨IsSquare n)
    72‚Üí    (hP : (largestPrimeFactor n : ‚ĄĚ) > Real.sqrt (2 * (n : ‚ĄĚ)) + 1) :
    73‚Üí    erdos841T n = largestPrimeFactor n := by
    74‚Üí  sorry
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/--
    77‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 841** ‚ÄĒ Selfridge's upper bound [ErSe92]:
    78‚Üí
    79‚ÜíIf $P(n) \leq \sqrt{2n} + 1$, then $t_n \ll \sqrt{n}$. Formally: there exists $C > 0$ such that
    80‚Üífor all non-square $n \geq 2$ with $P(n) \leq \sqrt{2n} + 1$, we have $t_n \leq C \cdot \sqrt{n}$.
    81‚Üí-/
    82‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    83‚Üítheorem erdos_841.variants.selfridge_upper :
    84‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• 2 ‚Üí ¬¨IsSquare n ‚Üí
    85‚Üí    (largestPrimeFactor n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ Real.sqrt (2 * (n : ‚ĄĚ)) + 1 ‚Üí
    86‚Üí    (erdos841T n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ C * Real.sqrt (n : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    87‚Üí  sorry
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí/--
    90‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 841** ‚ÄĒ Bui‚ÄďPratt‚ÄďZaharescu lower bound [BPZ24]:
    91‚Üí
    92‚ÜíFor all non-square $n \geq N_0$,
    93‚Üí$$t_n \geq C \cdot (\log \log n)^{6/5} \cdot (\log \log \log n)^{-1/5}.$$
    94‚Üí-/
    95‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    96‚Üítheorem erdos_841.variants.bpz_lower :
    97‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí ¬¨IsSquare n ‚Üí
    98‚Üí    (erdos841T n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č•
    99‚Üí      C * (Real.log (Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ))) ^ ((6 : ‚ĄĚ) / 5) *
   100‚Üí        (Real.log (Real.log (Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ)))) ^ (-(1 : ‚ĄĚ) / 5) := by
   101‚Üí  sorry
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üíend Erdos841
   104‚Üí

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 844
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/844](https://www.erdosproblems.com/844)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs and S√°rk√∂zy [Er92b]. Proved affirmatively by Weisenberg,
    25‚Üíand independently by Alexeev, Mixon, and Sawin [AMS25].
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Er92b] ErdŇĎs, P. and S√°rk√∂zy, A., on products of elements not being squarefree (1992).
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[AMS25] Alexeev, B., Mixon, D., and Sawin, W. (2025).
    30‚Üí-/
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üínamespace Erdos844
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/-- The candidate extremal set for ErdŇĎs Problem 844: all numbers in
    35‚Üí$\{1, \ldots, N\}$ that are either even or not squarefree. -/
    36‚Üínoncomputable def extremalSet (N : ‚Ąē) : Finset ‚Ąē :=
    37‚Üí  (Finset.Icc 1 N).filter (fun n => Even n ‚ą® ¬¨Squarefree n)
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/--
    40‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 844** [Er92b]:
    41‚Üí
    42‚ÜíLet $A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ be such that for all $a, b \in A$, the product $ab$ is not
    43‚Üísquarefree. Then $|A| \leq |\mathrm{extremalSet}(N)|$.
    44‚Üí
    45‚ÜíEquivalently, the maximum is achieved by the set of all even numbers together
    46‚Üíwith all odd non-squarefree numbers in $\{1, \ldots, N\}$.
    47‚Üí
    48‚ÜíProved by Weisenberg, and independently by Alexeev, Mixon, and Sawin [AMS25].
    49‚Üí-/
    50‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    51‚Üítheorem erdos_844 :
    52‚Üí    answer(True) ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ (N : ‚Ąē) (A : Finset ‚Ąē),
    53‚Üí      A ‚äÜ Finset.Icc 1 N ‚Üí
    54‚Üí      (‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą A, ¬¨Squarefree (a * b)) ‚Üí
    55‚Üí      A.card ‚Č§ (extremalSet N).card := by
    56‚Üí  sorry
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üíend Erdos844
    59‚Üí

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 991
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/991](https://www.erdosproblems.com/991)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíSuppose $A = \{w_1, \ldots, w_n\} \subset S^2$ maximises $\prod_{i<j} |w_i - w_j|$ over all
    25‚Üípossible sets of size $n$ (such sets are called Fekete points).
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíIs it true that
    28‚Üí$$
    29‚Üí  \max_C \left| |A \cap C| - \alpha_C \cdot n \right| = o(n),
    30‚Üí$$
    31‚Üíwhere the maximum is taken over all spherical caps $C$ and $\alpha_C$ is the area
    32‚Üíof $C$ (normalised so that the entire sphere has area $1$)?
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíThis was proved. Brauchart [Br08] showed $O(n^{3/4})$ and Marzo‚ÄďMas [MaMa21]
    35‚Üíimproved this to $O(n^{2/3})$.
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[Br08] Brauchart, J. S., *Optimal discrete Riesz energy and discrepancy*,
    38‚ÜíUniform Distribution Theory 3 (2008), no. 1, 47‚Äď78.
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí[MaMa21] Marzo, J. and Mas, A., *Discrepancy of minimal Riesz energy points*,
    41‚ÜíJ. Approx. Theory 264 (2021), 105538.
    42‚Üí-/
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üíopen Classical Finset BigOperators
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üínamespace Erdos991
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- The product of pairwise distances of a finite set of points. -/
    49‚Üínoncomputable def pairwiseDistProd (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 3))) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    50‚Üí  ‚ąŹ p ‚ąą P.offDiag, ‚ÄĖp.1 - p.2‚ÄĖ
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/-- A finite set of points on $S^2$ maximises the pairwise distance product
    53‚Üíamong all sets of the same cardinality on $S^2$. -/
    54‚Üídef IsMaxPairwiseDist (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 3))) : Prop :=
    55‚Üí  (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, ‚ÄĖp‚ÄĖ = 1) ‚ąß
    56‚Üí  ‚ąÄ (Q : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 3))),
    57‚Üí    (‚ąÄ q ‚ąą Q, ‚ÄĖq‚ÄĖ = 1) ‚Üí Q.card = P.card ‚Üí
    58‚Üí    pairwiseDistProd Q ‚Č§ pairwiseDistProd P
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/--
    61‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 991 [Er64b]:
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíIf $A = \{w_1, \ldots, w_n\} \subset S^2$ are Fekete points (maximising the product of
    64‚Üípairwise distances), then the spherical cap discrepancy of $A$ is $o(n)$:
    65‚Üífor every $\varepsilon > 0$, if $n$ is large enough and $P$ is any set of $n$ Fekete points,
    66‚Üíthen for every spherical cap $C(v,t) = \{x \in S^2 : \langle x,v \rangle \geq t\}$,
    67‚Üí$$
    68‚Üí  \left| |P \cap C(v,t)| - \frac{1-t}{2} \cdot n \right| \leq \varepsilon \cdot n.
    69‚Üí$$
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíHere $\frac{1-t}{2}$ is the normalised surface area of the cap $C(v,t)$ on $S^2$.
    72‚Üí-/
    73‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 31 52]
    74‚Üítheorem erdos_991 :
    75‚Üí    answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    76‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    77‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 3))),
    78‚Üí      IsMaxPairwiseDist P ‚Üí
    79‚Üí      P.card ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    80‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (v : EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 3)) (t : ‚ĄĚ),
    81‚Üí        ‚ÄĖv‚ÄĖ = 1 ‚Üí -1 ‚Č§ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ 1 ‚Üí
    82‚Üí        |((P.filter (fun x => @inner ‚ĄĚ _ _ x v ‚Č• t)).card : ‚ĄĚ) -
    83‚Üí          (1 - t) / 2 * (P.card : ‚ĄĚ)| ‚Č§ őĶ * (P.card : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    84‚Üí  sorry
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üíend Erdos991
    87‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 957
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/957](https://www.erdosproblems.com/957)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $A \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ be a set of size $n$ and let $\{d_1 < \ldots < d_k\}$
    25‚Üíbe the set of distinct distances determined by $A$. Let $f(d)$ be the number of
    26‚Üí(unordered) pairs of points at distance $d$. Is it true that
    27‚Üí$$f(d_1) f(d_k) \leq \left(\frac{9}{8} + o(1)\right) n^2?$$
    28‚Üí
    29‚ÜíA question of ErdŇĎs and Pach [ErPa90]. An unpublished construction of Makai
    30‚Üíshows that this would be the best possible. Solved by Dumitrescu [Du19], who
    31‚Üíproved $f(d_1) f(d_k) \leq \frac{9}{8} n^2 + O(n)$.
    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíSee also problems #132 and #756.
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[ErPa90] ErdŇĎs, P. and Pach, J., _Variation on the theme of repeated distances_ (1990).
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[Du19] Dumitrescu, A. (2019).
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üíopen Classical
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üínamespace Erdos957
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- The number of unordered pairs of distinct points in $A$ at Euclidean
    45‚Üí    distance $d$. Computed as the number of ordered pairs divided by 2. -/
    46‚Üínoncomputable def distFrequency (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))) (d : ‚ĄĚ) : ‚Ąē :=
    47‚Üí  ((A √óňĘ A).filter (fun p => p.1 ‚Č† p.2 ‚ąß dist p.1 p.2 = d)).card / 2
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/--
    50‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 957 [ErPa90] (proved by Dumitrescu [Du19]):
    51‚ÜíFor every $\varepsilon > 0$, for all sufficiently large finite point sets
    52‚Üí$A \subset \mathbb{R}^2$, the product of the frequency of the minimum distance
    53‚Üíand the frequency of the maximum distance satisfies
    54‚Üí$f(d_{\min}) \cdot f(d_{\max}) \leq (9/8 + \varepsilon) \cdot |A|^2$.
    55‚Üí-/
    56‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
    57‚Üítheorem erdos_957 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    58‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < őĶ ‚Üí
    59‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))), N‚āÄ ‚Č§ A.card ‚Üí
    60‚Üí    ‚ąÄ d‚āĀ dk : ‚ĄĚ,
    61‚Üí    (‚ąÉ p q : EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2), p ‚ąą A ‚ąß q ‚ąą A ‚ąß p ‚Č† q ‚ąß dist p q = d‚āĀ) ‚Üí
    62‚Üí    (‚ąÄ p q : EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2), p ‚ąą A ‚Üí q ‚ąą A ‚Üí p ‚Č† q ‚Üí d‚āĀ ‚Č§ dist p q) ‚Üí
    63‚Üí    (‚ąÉ p q : EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2), p ‚ąą A ‚ąß q ‚ąą A ‚ąß p ‚Č† q ‚ąß dist p q = dk) ‚Üí
    64‚Üí    (‚ąÄ p q : EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2), p ‚ąą A ‚Üí q ‚ąą A ‚Üí dist p q ‚Č§ dk) ‚Üí
    65‚Üí    (distFrequency A d‚āĀ : ‚ĄĚ) * (distFrequency A dk : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§
    66‚Üí      (9 / 8 + őĶ) * (A.card : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 := by
    67‚Üí  sorry
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üíend Erdos957

    35‚Üí/-- The prime counting function $\pi(n)$: the number of primes $\leq n$. -/

    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- $f(k,n)$ is the smallest integer $r$ such that in any $A \subseteq \{1,\ldots,n\}$ of size
    45‚Üí$|A| = k$, there exist $r$ primes $p_1,\ldots,p_r$ such that more than $r$ elements of $A$ are

    49‚Üí    ‚ąÉ P : Finset ‚Ąē, (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, Nat.Prime p) ‚ąß P.card = r ‚ąß

    24‚ÜíLet $F(n)$ be the maximum possible size of a subset $A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, n\}$ such that
    25‚Üí$a \nmid bc$ whenever $a, b, c \in A$ with $a \neq b$ and $a \neq c$. Is there a constant $C$

    34‚Üí    \leq \pi(n) + c_2 \cdot n^{2/3} \cdot (\log n)^{-2}.

    21‚Üísection Monoid

    24‚Üídef Powerfree (k : ‚Ąē) (m : M) : Prop :=
    25‚Üí  ‚ąÄ ‚¶Éx : M‚¶Ą, x ^ k ‚ą£ m ‚Üí IsUnit x

    27‚Üítheorem Powerfree.of_le {a b : ‚Ąē} (hab : a ‚Č§ b) (hm : Powerfree a m) :
    28‚Üí  Powerfree b m := fun x hx => hm (dvd_of_mul_right_dvd (pow_mul_pow_sub x hab ‚Ėł hx))
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üítheorem Powerfree.of_dvd (hrm : r ‚ą£ m) (hm : Powerfree k m) :
    31‚Üí    Powerfree k r := fun _ hx => hm (hx.trans hrm)

    34‚Üí  ‚ü®fun h => h.of_dvd hrm.dvd', fun h => h.of_dvd hrm.dvd‚ü©

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Primitive elements of a set with respect to divisibility
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíAn element `n` is *primitive* in a set `S ‚äÜ ‚Ąē` if `n ‚ąą S` and no proper divisor of `n` is in `S`.
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíThis concept appears naturally when studying sequences closed under "divisor inheritance" -
    25‚Üíif every member `n` of a sequence has the property that `m * n` is also in the sequence
    26‚Üífor coprime `m`, then the primitive elements generate the entire sequence.
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí## Main definitions
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí* `Set.IsPrimitive`: A natural number `n` is primitive in `S` if `n ‚ąą S` and
    31‚Üí  `n.properDivisors` is disjoint from `S`.
    32‚Üí* `Set.primitives`: The set of primitive elements of `S`.
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí## Main results
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí* `Set.IsPrimitive.mem`: A primitive element is in the set.
    37‚Üí* `Set.IsPrimitive.not_mem_of_properDivisor`: Proper divisors of a primitive element are not
    38‚Üí  in the set.
    39‚Üí* `Set.one_isPrimitive_iff`: `1` is primitive in `S` iff `1 ‚ąą S`.
    40‚Üí* `Set.prime_isPrimitive_iff`: A prime `p` is primitive in `S` iff `p ‚ąą S` and `1 ‚ąČ S`.
    41‚Üí-/
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üínamespace Set
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- An element `n` is *primitive* in a set `S ‚äÜ ‚Ąē` if `n ‚ąą S` and no proper divisor of `n`
    46‚Üíis in `S`. -/
    47‚Üídef IsPrimitive (S : Set ‚Ąē) (n : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    48‚Üí  n ‚ąą S ‚ąß Disjoint (n.properDivisors : Set ‚Ąē) S
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- The set of primitive elements of `S`. -/
    51‚Üídef primitives (S : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    52‚Üí  {n | S.IsPrimitive n}
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üítheorem IsPrimitive.mem {S : Set ‚Ąē} {n : ‚Ąē} (h : S.IsPrimitive n) : n ‚ąą S := h.1
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üítheorem IsPrimitive.disjoint_properDivisors {S : Set ‚Ąē} {n : ‚Ąē} (h : S.IsPrimitive n) :
    57‚Üí    Disjoint (n.properDivisors : Set ‚Ąē) S := h.2
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üítheorem IsPrimitive.not_mem_of_properDivisor {S : Set ‚Ąē} {n d : ‚Ąē} (h : S.IsPrimitive n)
    60‚Üí    (hd : d ‚ąą n.properDivisors) : d ‚ąČ S :=
    61‚Üí  Set.disjoint_left.mp h.2 hd
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üítheorem IsPrimitive.not_mem_of_dvd_of_lt {S : Set ‚Ąē} {n d : ‚Ąē} (h : S.IsPrimitive n)
    64‚Üí    (hdvd : d ‚ą£ n) (hlt : d < n) : d ‚ąČ S :=
    65‚Üí  h.not_mem_of_properDivisor (Nat.mem_properDivisors.mpr ‚ü®hdvd, hlt‚ü©)
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üítheorem isPrimitive_iff {S : Set ‚Ąē} {n : ‚Ąē} :
    68‚Üí    S.IsPrimitive n ‚ÜĒ n ‚ąą S ‚ąß ‚ąÄ d ‚ąą n.properDivisors, d ‚ąČ S := by
    69‚Üí  simp only [IsPrimitive, Set.disjoint_left, Finset.mem_coe]
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üítheorem isPrimitive_iff' {S : Set ‚Ąē} {n : ‚Ąē} (_hn : 0 < n) :
    72‚Üí    S.IsPrimitive n ‚ÜĒ n ‚ąą S ‚ąß ‚ąÄ d, d ‚ą£ n ‚Üí d < n ‚Üí d ‚ąČ S := by
    73‚Üí  rw [isPrimitive_iff]
    74‚Üí  constructor <;> intro ‚ü®hmem, hdiv‚ü©
    75‚Üí  ¬∑ exact ‚ü®hmem, fun d hd hlt => hdiv d (Nat.mem_properDivisors.mpr ‚ü®hd, hlt‚ü©)‚ü©
    76‚Üí  ¬∑ exact ‚ü®hmem, fun d hd => hdiv d (Nat.mem_properDivisors.mp hd).1 (Nat.mem_properDivisors.mp hd).2‚ü©
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üítheorem mem_primitives_iff {S : Set ‚Ąē} {n : ‚Ąē} : n ‚ąą S.primitives ‚ÜĒ S.IsPrimitive n := Iff.rfl
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí@[simp]
    81‚Üítheorem primitives_subset {S : Set ‚Ąē} : S.primitives ‚äÜ S := fun _ h => h.mem
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üítheorem one_isPrimitive_iff {S : Set ‚Ąē} : S.IsPrimitive 1 ‚ÜĒ 1 ‚ąą S := by
    84‚Üí  simp [isPrimitive_iff, Nat.properDivisors_one]
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üítheorem prime_isPrimitive_iff {S : Set ‚Ąē} {p : ‚Ąē} (hp : p.Prime) :
    87‚Üí    S.IsPrimitive p ‚ÜĒ p ‚ąą S ‚ąß 1 ‚ąČ S := by
    88‚Üí  simp [isPrimitive_iff, hp.properDivisors]
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üíend Set

    23‚Üí/--
    24‚ÜíA natural number $n$ is said to be $k$-full (or $k$-powerful) if for every prime factor $p$ of $n$,
    25‚Üíthe $k$-th power $p^k$ also divides $n$.

    27‚Üídef Full (k : ‚Ąē) (n : ‚Ąē) : Prop := ‚ąÄ p ‚ąą n.primeFactors, p^k ‚ą£ n
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üíinstance Full.decide : ‚ąÄ k n, Decidable (Full k n) := by

    31‚Üí  dsimp [Full]

    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 156
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/156](https://www.erdosproblems.com/156)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA question of ErdŇĎs, S√°rk√∂zy, and S√≥s [ESS94]. Ruzsa [Ru98b] constructed a maximal Sidon set
    25‚Üíof size $\ll (N \log N)^{1/3}$, but whether $O(N^{1/3})$ is achievable remains open.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[ESS94] ErdŇĎs, P., S√°rk√∂zy, A., and S√≥s, V. T.
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[Ru98b] Ruzsa, I. Z.
    30‚Üí-/
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üíopen Filter
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen scoped Topology Real
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos156
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- A finite set of natural numbers is a Sidon set (also called a $B_2$ set) if all
    39‚Üípairwise sums $a + b$ (allowing $a = b$) are distinct: whenever $a + b = c + d$
    40‚Üíwith $a, b, c, d \in A$, we have $\{a, b\} = \{c, d\}$ as multisets. -/
    41‚Üídef IsSidonSet (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    42‚Üí  ‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ d ‚ąą A,
    43‚Üí    a + b = c + d ‚Üí (a = c ‚ąß b = d) ‚ą® (a = d ‚ąß b = c)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- A Sidon set $A \subseteq \{0, \ldots, N-1\}$ is maximal (in $\{0, \ldots, N-1\}$) if no
    46‚Üíelement of $\{0, \ldots, N-1\} \setminus A$ can be added to $A$ while preserving the Sidon
    47‚Üíproperty. -/
    48‚Üídef IsMaximalSidonSet (N : ‚Ąē) (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    49‚Üí  A ‚äÜ Finset.range N ‚ąß
    50‚Üí  IsSidonSet A ‚ąß
    51‚Üí  ‚ąÄ n ‚ąą Finset.range N, n ‚ąČ A ‚Üí ¬¨IsSidonSet (insert n A)
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/--
    54‚ÜíErdŇĎs‚ÄďS√°rk√∂zy‚ÄďS√≥s Conjecture (Problem 156) [ESS94]:
    55‚Üí
    56‚ÜíDoes there exist a maximal Sidon set $A \subset \{1, \ldots, N\}$ of size $O(N^{1/3})$?
    57‚Üí
    58‚ÜíThe greedy algorithm produces a maximal Sidon set of size $\gg N^{1/3}$ (this is known).
    59‚ÜíRuzsa [Ru98b] constructed a maximal Sidon set of size $\ll (N \log N)^{1/3}$, which is
    60‚Üíclose but does not reach $O(N^{1/3})$.
    61‚Üí
    62‚ÜíFormalized as: there exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $N$,
    63‚Üíthere exists a maximal Sidon set $A \subseteq \{0, \ldots, N-1\}$ with
    64‚Üí$|A| \leq C \cdot N^{1/3}$.
    65‚Üí-/
    66‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    67‚Üítheorem erdos_156 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    68‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < C ‚ąß
    69‚Üí      ‚ąÄŠ∂† N : ‚Ąē in atTop,
    70‚Üí        ‚ąÉ A : Finset ‚Ąē, IsMaximalSidonSet N A ‚ąß
    71‚Üí          (A.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ C * (N : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ) / 3) := by
    72‚Üí  sorry
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üíend Erdos156

    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/157](https://www.erdosproblems.com/157)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíErdŇĎs, S√°rk√∂zy, and S√≥s asked whether there exists an infinite Sidon set that is also an
    25‚Üíasymptotic basis of order 3. This was answered affirmatively by Pilatte.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[ESS94] ErdŇĎs, P., S√°rk√∂zy, A. and S√≥s, V. T., *On sum sets of Sidon sets*. J. Number Theory
    28‚Üí(1994).
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[Er94b] ErdŇĎs, P., *Some of my favourite problems in various branches of combinatorics*.
    31‚ÜíMatematiche (Catania) (1994).
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[Pi23] Pilatte, C., *Sidon sets are asymptotic bases of order 3*. (2023).
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen Filter Set
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace Erdos157
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- An infinite set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is a Sidon set ($B_2$ set) if all pairwise sums
    41‚Üíare distinct: whenever $a + b = c + d$ with $a, b, c, d \in A$, we have
    42‚Üí$\{a, b\} = \{c, d\}$ as multisets (i.e. either $a = c$ and $b = d$, or
    43‚Üí$a = d$ and $b = c$). -/
    44‚Üídef IsInfiniteSidonSet (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    45‚Üí  Set.Infinite A ‚ąß
    46‚Üí  ‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ d ‚ąą A,
    47‚Üí    a + b = c + d ‚Üí (a = c ‚ąß b = d) ‚ą® (a = d ‚ąß b = c)
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- A set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an asymptotic basis of order $3$ if every sufficiently
    50‚Üílarge natural number can be represented as a sum of exactly $3$ elements
    51‚Üí(with repetition allowed) from $A$. -/
    52‚Üídef IsAsymptoticBasisOrder3 (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    53‚Üí  ‚ąÄŠ∂† n : ‚Ąē in atTop, ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ c ‚ąą A, n = a + b + c
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/--
    56‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 157 [ESS94, Er94b]:
    57‚Üí
    58‚ÜíDoes there exist an infinite Sidon set which is an asymptotic basis of order $3$?
    59‚Üí
    60‚ÜíA set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is a Sidon set if all pairwise sums $a + b$ ($a, b \in A$)
    61‚Üíare distinct. A set $A$ is an asymptotic basis of order $3$ if every sufficiently large
    62‚Üíinteger is the sum of $3$ elements from $A$.
    63‚Üí
    64‚ÜíAnswered YES by Pilatte [Pi23].
    65‚Üí-/
    66‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    67‚Üítheorem erdos_157 :
    68‚Üí    answer(True) ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ A : Set ‚Ąē, IsInfiniteSidonSet A ‚ąß IsAsymptoticBasisOrder3 A := by
    69‚Üí  sorry
    70‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 154
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/154](https://www.erdosproblems.com/154)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $A \subset \{1, \ldots, N\}$ be a Sidon set with $|A| \sim N^{1/2}$.
    25‚ÜíMust the sumset $A + A$ be well-distributed over all small moduli?
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[ESS94] ErdŇĎs, P., S√°rk√∂zy, A., and S√≥s, V. T., 1994.
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[Li98] Lindstr√∂m, B., 1998.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[Ko99] Kolountzakis, M., 1999.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen Filter
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen scoped Topology Real
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace Erdos154
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- A finite set of natural numbers is a Sidon set (also called a $B_2$ set) if all
    41‚Üípairwise sums $a + b$ (allowing $a = b$) are distinct: whenever $a + b = c + d$
    42‚Üíwith $a, b, c, d \in A$, we have $\{a, b\} = \{c, d\}$ as multisets. Equivalently,
    43‚Üíall differences $a - b$ with $a \neq b$ and $a, b \in A$ are distinct. -/
    44‚Üídef IsSidonSet (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    45‚Üí  ‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ d ‚ąą A,
    46‚Üí    a + b = c + d ‚Üí (a = c ‚ąß b = d) ‚ą® (a = d ‚ąß b = c)
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- The sumset $A + A = \{a + b \mid a, b \in A\}$. -/
    49‚Üídef sumset (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Finset ‚Ąē := Finset.image‚āā (¬∑ + ¬∑) A A
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/-- The fraction of elements in a finite set of naturals that are congruent to $r$ modulo $m$. -/
    52‚Üínoncomputable def modFraction (m r : ‚Ąē) (S : Finset ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    53‚Üí  ((S.filter (fun n => n % m = r)).card : ‚ĄĚ) / (S.card : ‚ĄĚ)
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/--
    56‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem \#154 [ESS94]:
    57‚Üí
    58‚ÜíLet $A \subset \{1, \ldots, N\}$ be a Sidon set with $|A| \sim N^{1/2}$. Must $A + A$ be
    59‚Üíwell-distributed over all small moduli? In particular, must about half
    60‚Üíthe elements of $A + A$ be even and half odd?
    61‚Üí
    62‚ÜíProved in the affirmative. Lindstr√∂m [Li98] showed that $A$ itself is
    63‚Üíwell-distributed modulo small integers (e.g. $|A \cap \{\text{evens}\}| \approx |A|/2$),
    64‚Üísubsequently strengthened by Kolountzakis [Ko99]. The extension to $A + A$
    65‚Üífollows immediately from the Sidon property: if $A$ has $e$ even and $o$ odd
    66‚Üíelements, then $A + A$ has exactly $e(e+1)/2 + o(o+1)/2$ even elements
    67‚Üíand $eo$ odd elements (all distinct by the Sidon property), and the
    68‚Üídistribution is approximately $1/2$ each when $e \approx o \approx |A|/2$.
    69‚Üí
    70‚ÜíFormalized as: for any sequence $(A_n)_n$ of Sidon sets $A_n \subset \{0, \ldots, n\}$
    71‚Üíwith $|A_n| / \sqrt{n} \to 1$ as $n \to \infty$, and any fixed modulus $m \geq 1$ and
    72‚Üíresidue $0 \leq r < m$, the fraction of elements of $A_n + A_n$ in residue
    73‚Üíclass $r \bmod m$ tends to $1/m$.
    74‚Üí-/
    75‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    76‚Üítheorem erdos_154 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    77‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (A : ‚Ąē ‚Üí Finset ‚Ąē),
    78‚Üí      (‚ąÄ n, IsSidonSet (A n)) ‚Üí
    79‚Üí      (‚ąÄ n, (A n) ‚äÜ Finset.range (n + 1)) ‚Üí
    80‚Üí      Tendsto (fun n => ((A n).card : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.sqrt n) atTop (ūĚďĚ 1) ‚Üí
    81‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (m : ‚Ąē), 1 ‚Č§ m ‚Üí
    82‚Üí        ‚ąÄ r < m,
    83‚Üí          Tendsto (fun n => modFraction m r (sumset (A n))) atTop (ūĚďĚ (1 / (m : ‚ĄĚ))) := by
    84‚Üí  sorry
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üíend Erdos154
